name: Pontevedra - tour of the 4 Peaks and Rio Lerez
name_original: Pontevedra - vuelta de los 4 Picos y Rio Lerez
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Litoral traverse a diverse landscape that includes extensive coastlines, river estuaries, and rolling inland plains. The region features a mix of sandy beaches, dramatic cliffs, and tranquil lagoons, alongside cork oak forests, olive groves, and vineyards. Cyclists encounter quiet roads, dedicated bike paths, and wooden boardwalks, with varied terrain ranging from mostly flat coastal sections to routes winding through hills. This area offers a blend of natural beauty and varied topography suitable for road cycling. Best road cycling routes around Litoral The most popular road cycling route is Vigo - velodrome through Ramallosa, a 21.8 miles (35.0 km) trail that takes 1 hour 28 minutes to complete. This easy route offers a gentle ride through the local area. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Vigo - Gondomar - return via Zamáns Reservoir and University, a moderate 30.7 miles (49.5 km) path. This route includes a return via the Zamáns Reservoir and the University, offering varied scenery. Local road cyclists also love the Baiona Sierra and coast return, a 45.7 miles (73.5 km) trail leading through the Baiona Sierra and along the coast, often completed in about 3 hours 54 minutes. Road cycling around Litoral is defined by its coastal paths, river estuaries, and rolling inland plains. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy coastal rides to more challenging routes with significant elevation gain. The routes in Litoral are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 380 reviews. More than 18,000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Litoral's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available in Litoral?
A: Litoral offers a wide selection of road cycling routes, with over 680 tours available. These routes cater to various skill levels, from easy coastal rides to more challenging inland ascents.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Litoral?
A: The Litoral region presents a diverse landscape for road cyclists. You'll find extensive coastlines with sandy beaches and dramatic cliffs, tranquil lagoons, and river estuaries. Inland, routes traverse rolling plains, cork oak forests, olive groves, and vineyards. The terrain varies from mostly flat coastal sections, often with dedicated bike paths and wooden boardwalks, to routes winding through hills with significant elevation gain.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Litoral?
A: The Litoral region, particularly the Alentejo Litoral and Northern Littoral Natural Park, is generally pleasant for cycling for much of the year. Spring and autumn offer mild temperatures and less crowded routes, making them ideal. Summer can be warm, especially inland, but coastal breezes provide some relief. Winter cycling is also an option, particularly in the milder southern parts, though some coastal areas might experience more wind and rain.

Q: How do other cyclists rate the road cycling experience in Litoral?
A: The road cycling routes in Litoral are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an average rating of 4.5 stars from over 380 reviews. Cyclists frequently praise the region's varied terrain, stunning coastal views, and the blend of natural beauty with charming villages and historical sites.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Litoral?
A: Yes, Litoral offers numerous routes perfect for beginners. There are 68 easy routes available, many of which are found along the coast or on dedicated bike paths. A great option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e969008856/vigo-velodrome-through-ramallosa" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vigo - velodrome through Ramallosa&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 21.8-mile (35.0 km) trail that provides a gentle introduction to the area's cycling.

Q: Are there challenging road cycling routes for experienced cyclists in Litoral?
A: Absolutely. For experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, Litoral has 278 difficult routes. These often involve significant elevation gains and longer distances, taking you through hilly inland areas or along more demanding coastal sections. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e961694312/baiona-sierra-and-coast-return" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Baiona Sierra and coast return&lt;/a&gt;, a 45.7-mile (73.5 km) trail with over 1000m of ascent.

Q: Can I find circular road cycling routes in Litoral?
A: Yes, many road cycling routes in Litoral are designed as circular tours, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e968940786/vigo-tui-circular-via-montferro-to-the-rio-mino" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vigo - Tui - circular via Montferro to the Rio Miño&lt;/a&gt; offers a substantial 76.6 km loop, providing varied scenery and a rewarding ride.

Q: What natural features can I discover along road cycling routes in Litoral?
A: The Litoral region is rich in natural beauty. Cyclists can encounter dramatic cliffs, sandy beaches, and protected lagoons like Santo André and Melides. The Southwest Alentejo and Vicentine Coast Natural Park is known for its diverse ecosystems. Further north, the Northern Littoral Natural Park boasts extensive white sand dunes and significant river estuaries like the Cávado and Neiva, which are vital bird habitats. You might also discover waterfalls such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2030121" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barosa Waterfall and Mills&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/566061" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Waterfall on the Senda del Agua&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any lighthouses or historical sites to see while cycling in Litoral?
A: Yes, the Litoral region offers several interesting landmarks. Along the coast, you can spot lighthouses such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3006175" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cabo Home Lighthouse&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3006184" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Punta Robaleira Lighthouse&lt;/a&gt;. Historically, the city of Sines, birthplace of Vasco da Gama, offers cultural insights, and offshore, Ilha do Pessegueiro features a 16th-century fort and Roman ruins. The Troia Peninsula also holds significant Roman ruins.

Q: Are there any lakes or reservoirs accessible from road cycling routes in Litoral?
A: Yes, several routes pass by or near beautiful bodies of water. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e968974304/vigo-gondomar-return-via-zamans-reservoir-and-university" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vigo - Gondomar - return via Zamáns Reservoir and University&lt;/a&gt; route includes a scenic return via the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5198910" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Zamáns Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;. Other natural water features include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/921667" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Poza da Moura&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1400940" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lake Cotorredondo&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I find family-friendly road cycling routes in Litoral?
A: While road cycling routes are generally more suited for adults, many of the easier, flatter coastal paths within Litoral can be enjoyed by families with older children or those comfortable with longer distances. The Northern Littoral Natural Park, for instance, integrates with the Ecovia do Litoral Norte, offering mostly flat cycling along dedicated bike paths and wooden boardwalks, which can be suitable for a family outing.

Q: Are there routes that explore the Pontevedra Peninsula?
A: Yes, you can explore the scenic Pontevedra Peninsula by road bike. A notable route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e969123758/pontevedra-peninsula-tour-around-o-grove-and-sanxenxo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pontevedra Peninsula - tour around O Grove and Sanxenxo&lt;/a&gt;, which covers nearly 60 km and offers beautiful coastal views and a moderate challenge.

Q: Where can I find more information about the Alentejo Litoral region for cycling?
A: For additional insights into cycling opportunities and the natural beauty of the Alentejo Litoral, you can visit official tourism resources. The region is part of long-distance cycling trails like the Rota Vicentina. More information can be found on sites such as &lt;a href="https://www.visitalentejo.pt/en/alentejo/nature/cycling/" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;visitalentejo.pt&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.visitportugal.com/en/content/alentejo-coast" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;visitportugal.com&lt;/a&gt;.
tips: A quiet road running alongside the coast heading West out of Marin. The bike path is bi-directional while the road is one way only. However the bike path was heavily litter with leaves and branches after stroms earlier in the week. The road however was quiet and smooth.
Shorter climb up to a lovely park and lake. Some nice looking cafes at the top.
Smooth zigzag climb of 1.65km with an average gradient of 5.5%. Perfect for those short training efforts
Descend 350m towards Santa Maria de Xeve. along a 6.5 km road
